work_unit: AE-H-001 save-checkpoint.py run() shell=True injection surface elimination
situation: audit-2026-07-08-043 finding AE-H-001 (high): save-checkpoint.py:27 run() uses subprocess.check_output(shell=True). Current 3 call sites are hardcoded git literals so no live exploit, but API design invites future vuln if any caller concatenates external input.
action: Refactored run() to accept list[str] and call subprocess with shell=False. Windows path wrapped via ['cmd','/c',...] to preserve %VAR% expansion for git format strings. Updated all 3 internal call sites (git branch/status/log) to list form. Syntax-checked. Committed + pushed on isolated worktree branch auto/opt-hooks-1783388352.
outcome_evidence: Commit 088f7ef on auto/opt-hooks-1783388352; pushed to origin https://code.alibaba-inc.com/xtool/autonomous-engine.git; python3 -c ast.parse OK; diff shows shell=True removed, 3 call sites converted to list literals.
next_suggestion: Next round dispatch AE-H-002 (watchdog.sh f-string path injection) — same project, same audit cycle. After that AE-M-001 non-atomic checkpoint write.

audit_findings.finding: run() helper uses subprocess.check_output(shell=True); design-level injection surface even though current callers are safe literals
audit_findings.remediation: Switch to shell=False + list-mode argv; wrap Windows calls via ['cmd','/c',...]
